questions.title: Watcher choice
questions.rationale: Adopt Chokidar now as the daemon watcher substrate; reject raw fs.watch and direct fsevents because the repo already ships Chokidar and has watcher behavior patterns/tests that address platform and editor-save hazards.

questions.title: Scope of change detection
questions.rationale: Adopt a narrow graph-index trigger on graph-metadata.json now, and pair it with targeted derived-metadata invalidation for SKILL.md, derived.source_docs, and derived.key_files.

questions.title: Incremental vs full re-index
questions.rationale: Use the existing TypeScript SQLite hash-aware incremental indexer as the daemon default, with full re-index reserved for explicit recovery or schema maintenance.

questions.title: Update transaction model
questions.rationale: Adopt the existing single-SQLite-transaction graph update as the authoritative consistency boundary and publish freshness/generation/cache invalidation only after commit.

questions.title: Daemon lifecycle
questions.rationale: Adopt the current MCP-owned lifecycle with startup scan, opt-in in-process watcher, graceful shutdown, freshness-gated hooks, and fail-open prompt behavior.

questions.title: Resource budget
questions.rationale: The narrow metadata-only daemon budget is acceptable after fixing watcher details, but broad watching needs resource benchmarks before default adoption.

questions.title: Sanitization at re-index
questions.rationale: Apply the existing renderer/shared-payload sanitizer at re-index before storing or publishing advisor-visible skill metadata.

questions.title: Failure modes
questions.rationale: Adopt a failure-mode contract with stable-write handling, benign ENOENT races, WAL caveats, SQLITE_BUSY handling, fail-open freshness, scan leases, and rebuild recovery.

questions.title: Extraction sources
questions.rationale: Adopt explicit metadata plus deterministic local content extraction now, while excluding commit messages from the first implementation slice.

questions.title: Extraction pipeline
questions.rationale: Adopt a deterministic hybrid pipeline with explicit signals, TypeScript n-gram/pattern extraction, and corpus-aware DF/IDF; keep embeddings offline or prototype-only.

questions.title: Sync model
questions.rationale: Preserve author-authored signals and write generated companions into schema-v2 graph-metadata.json.derived, with SQLite as the live advisor index.

questions.title: Precision safeguards
questions.rationale: Keep derived triggers in a separate lower-trust lane with caps, haircuts, and corpus-gated precision checks before default routing confidence can rise.

questions.title: Freshness trigger
questions.rationale: Use the A3 skill graph and content-hash SQLite index as durable invalidation authority, with a derived-provenance fingerprint for B1 inputs.

questions.title: Corpus stats
questions.rationale: Maintain repo-level DF/IDF stats as an index-time baseline, recomputed on startup and debounced graph or derived-input changes.

questions.title: Adversarial resilience
questions.rationale: Make adversarial scoring lanes explicit and enforce anti-stuffing caps and demotions before generated triggers become stronger.

questions.title: Available memory-MCP primitives
questions.rationale: The repo already ships the retrieval, trigger, causal, indexing, stats, health, and hybrid fusion primitives C1 asked for.

questions.title: Mapping memory concepts to advisor
questions.rationale: Skill metadata contains enough retrieval text and topology to project into memory-style channels, while memory lifecycle/governance/decay fields are inappropriate for canonical skills.

questions.title: Causal graph for skills
questions.rationale: Adopt causal-style skill traversal over skill_edges, not direct memory_causal_link reuse.

questions.title: Scoring fusion
questions.rationale: Adopt analytical fusion now; learned weighting remains shadow-only until C7/C8 calibration exists.

questions.title: Ambiguity handling
questions.rationale: Adopt causal-style skill-edge ambiguity handling upstream in scoring and uncertainty, while keeping render.ts deterministic and sanitized.

questions.title: Performance
questions.rationale: Do not adopt prompt-time embedding lookup on every advisor prompt now; keep exact cache, lexical, and skill-graph SQL work in the hot path.

questions.title: Training/tuning data
questions.rationale: Adopt the 200-prompt corpus as a frozen deterministic benchmark and stratified holdout dataset, but keep learned scoring shadow-only.

questions.title: Target accuracy
questions.rationale: Adopt 70% full-corpus and holdout top-1 gates for deterministic improvements, with stricter gates for prompt-time embedding or learned scoring.

questions.title: Current split inventory
questions.rationale: Adopt the split inventory as canonical Track D baseline.

questions.title: Migration target layout
questions.rationale: Adopt MCP-native layout with graph plane under lib/skill-graph and advisor runtime under lib/skill-advisor plus handlers/tools.

questions.title: MCP-tool surface
questions.rationale: Adopt advisor_recommend, advisor_status, and advisor_validate now; defer batch/benchmark tools.

questions.title: Subprocess elimination
questions.rationale: Adopt native TypeScript subprocess elimination in stages, with Python parity and compatibility gates.

questions.title: Cache + freshness sharing
questions.rationale: Adopt shared cache/freshness primitives while keeping advisor prompt-result caching as a domain-specific wrapper.

questions.title: Install/bootstrap
questions.rationale: Adopt install/bootstrap consolidation through docs, symlinks, verification, and rollback updates, not a new MCP server registration.

questions.title: Backward compat matrix
questions.rationale: Keep skill_advisor.py CLI modes, disable flags, and playbook scenarios as compatibility surfaces until parity and docs migration complete.

questions.title: Plugin relationship
questions.rationale: Keep the OpenCode plugin and bridge as adapters while routing the backend to native advisor behavior after consolidation.

roadmap.scope: Derived metadata and scoring-signal trust lanes

risks: Watcher misses, duplicate events, or atomic-write loops
SQLite writer contention or premature freshness publication
Premature semantic or learned scoring promotion
TypeScript port parity drift from Python
Measurement blind spots across safety and latency slices

safety.adversarial_stuffing: no stuffed skill passes default routing without direct evidence

privacy.hook_plugin_status: no raw prompt, prompt fingerprint, stdout, or stderr
